---
title: "Running BikeWise Online with Google Sheets"
author: "SebKrbs"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Running BikeWise Online with Google Sheets}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment  = "#>"
)
```

## Overview

BikeWise has two modes:

- **Offline** (`StartCycling()`): data is stored in local CSV files on your
  machine. Works immediately with no setup.
- **Online** (`StartCyclingOnline()`): data is stored in a Google Sheet. All
  sensitive data is encrypted before it reaches the sheet, so even if someone
  finds the sheet they cannot read the data.

This vignette walks through the three steps needed to get the online version
running.

---

## What you will need

Three environment variables in your `~/.Renviron` file:

| Variable | What it is |
|---|---|
| `BIKEWISE_SHEET_ID` | The ID of your Google Sheet |
| `BIKEWISE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT` | Path to a service account JSON key |
| `BIKEWISE_ENCRYPTION_KEY` | A secret string used to encrypt data |

Open `.Renviron` with:

```r
usethis::edit_r_environ()
```

---

## Step 1 — Create your Google Sheet

1. Go to [sheets.google.com](https://sheets.google.com) and create a new
   spreadsheet.

2. Create two tabs named **exactly** `users` and `locations` (case-sensitive).

3. In the `users` tab, add these column headers in row 1:

   ```
   username   password_hash   rain_tolerance   cycling_speed
   ```

4. In the `locations` tab, add these column headers in row 1:

   ```
   user   label   address   lat   lon   display_name
   ```

5. Copy the sheet ID from the URL —
   `docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/`**`THIS_PART`**`/edit`.

6. Add to `.Renviron`:

   ```
   BIKEWISE_SHEET_ID=paste-your-id-here
   ```

---

## Step 2 — Create a service account

A service account is a separate Google "robot" account that the app uses to
read and write your sheet. It has access only to the sheets you share with it
— not to your personal Google Drive.

1. Go to [console.cloud.google.com](https://console.cloud.google.com) and
   sign in with your Google account.

2. Click the project dropdown at the top and select **New Project**. Give it
   a name (e.g. `BikeWise`) and click **Create**.

3. In the search bar, type `Google Sheets API` and click **Enable**.

4. In the left sidebar go to **IAM & Admin → Service Accounts**. Click
   **+ Create Service Account**, name it (e.g. `bikewise-app`), and click
   **Done**.

5. Click the service account you just created, go to the **Keys** tab, click
   **Add Key → Create new key**, choose **JSON**, and click **Create**. A
   `.json` file will download to your computer.

   > **Important:** Never commit this file to git. Add `*.json` to your
   > `.gitignore` to be safe.

6. Open your Google Sheet, click **Share** (top right), and paste in the
   service account email address. It looks like
   `bikewise-app@your-project.iam.gserviceaccount.com` and is visible on the
   service accounts page. Give it **Editor** access and click **Send**.

7. Add to `.Renviron`:

   ```
   BIKEWISE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT=/path/to/downloaded-key.json
   ```

---

## Step 3 — Set an encryption key

Generate a random key by running this in R:

```r
paste(sample(c(letters, LETTERS, 0:9), 32, replace = TRUE), collapse = "")
```

Copy the output and add it to `.Renviron`:

```
BIKEWISE_ENCRYPTION_KEY=your-random-key-here
```

Save `.Renviron` and restart R.

---

## Running the app

```r
library(BikeWise)
StartCyclingOnline()
```

BikeWise will verify all three variables are set, authenticate with Google
Sheets using the service account, and open the app in your browser.

---

## Deploying to shinyapps.io

1. Create a free account at [shinyapps.io](https://www.shinyapps.io).

2. In R, install the deployment package:

   ```r
   install.packages("rsconnect")
   ```

3. In the shinyapps.io dashboard go to **Account → Tokens → Show** and copy
   the `rsconnect::setAccountInfo(...)` command. Run it in R once to link
   your machine.

4. Deploy the app:

   ```r
   rsconnect::deployApp(
     appDir = system.file("apps/bikewise-online", package = "BikeWise")
   )
   ```

5. In the shinyapps.io dashboard, open your app and go to
   **Settings → Environment Variables**. Add the three variables:

   ```
   BIKEWISE_SHEET_ID        your-sheet-id
   BIKEWISE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT paste the full contents of the JSON key file here
   BIKEWISE_ENCRYPTION_KEY  your-encryption-key
   ```

   For `BIKEWISE_SERVICE_ACCOUNT`, open the JSON file in a text editor,
   select all, and paste the contents as the value — not the file path.
   The authentication library detects automatically that it is a JSON string.

   Click **Restart** after saving the variables.
